intuition: Binary search works on sorted arrays by repeatedly halving the search space. Compare the target with the middle element — if equal, found it; if target is smaller, search the left half; if larger, search the right half.
approach: Maintain left and right pointers. While left <= right, compute mid = left + (right - left) / 2. If nums[mid] == target, return mid. If target < nums[mid], set right = mid - 1. Else set left = mid + 1. If the loop ends, return -1.

complexity.time: O(log n) — halve the search space each step

hints: The array is sorted — exploit this property.
What happens if you check the middle element?
Use left + (right - left) / 2 to avoid integer overflow.
commonMistakes: Using (left + right) / 2 which can overflow for large indices — use left + (right - left) / 2.
Using left < right instead of left <= right — misses the case where the target is the last remaining element.
